mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over 3 months, invalidating the recession signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer and investment demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle expansions, with a hit rate below 50% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 280.0, inflation expectations rise sharply, prompting markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and yields negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 280.0 are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of historical observations, breaking the inflation penalty link.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates severe labor market weakness that undermines consumer spending forecasts, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly expands corporate revenue bases and profit margins, creating a sustained upward pressure on S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across multiple business cycles, rejecting the earnings growth channel.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below the 2017 baseline of 100.0, it signals a contraction in manufacturing activity that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 100.0 are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, invalidating the contraction signal.
